---
title: "Sobre mí"
about:
  template: solana
  image: assets/img/LKProfilePhoto.png
  image-alt: "Foto de Tu Nombre"
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/juanperez
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/juanperez
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: assets/pdf/cv.pdf
---

## Technical milestones

### Systems Engineering & Game Mathematics (Senior Consultant) (2022 – Present)

- Domain-Specific Language (DSL) Design: Architected Costa Rica’s first comprehensive Game Mathematics framework, including a custom C++ interpreter-simulator and a proprietary scripting language. This reduced R&D cycles and enabled the mathematical modeling of 24+ commercial titles.

- Stochastic Large-Scale Simulation: Developed Docker-based simulation servers for massive probability models, ensuring statistical integrity and regulatory compliance for complex gaming mechanics.

### R&D in Digital Petrophysics (Shell Brazil / UFF) (2014-2018)

- Inverse Problem Engineering: Formulated and solved a novel inverse problem to recover surface relaxivity as a functional of pore size. By integrating Random Walk simulations with Tikhonov regularization, I achieved unprecedented accuracy in characterizing complex carbonate reservoirs.

- Comparative Multi-Physics Analysis: (As published in Journal of Petroleum Science and Engineering, 2020) Developed a formulation to convert NMR T2 distributions into pore size distributions using variable surface relaxivity. Conducted a landmark comparative study between NMR-derived data and image-based methods (Micro-CT), validating the predictive power of digital petrophysics against laboratory benchmarks.

- HPC Architecture: Engineered a Large-Scale Finite Element Method (FEM) solver using an Element-By-Element (EBE) approach in C++. This implementation bypassed global matrix assembly constraints, enabling the processing of high-resolution tomographic datasets (millions of nodes) that exceed standard RAM capacities.
